section.heading: General provisions

content.p: The Secretary may not make a grant under section 280c(a) of this title to a State unless the State agrees that not more than 10 percent of the grant will be expended for administrative expenses with respect to the grant.

subsection.heading: Description of intended use of grant
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary may not make a grant under section 280c(a) of this title to a State unless—

paragraph.content: the State submits to the Secretary a description of the purposes for which the State intends to expend the grant; and

paragraph.chapeau: such description provides information relating to the programs and activities to be supported and services to be provided, including—

subparagraph.content: the number of individuals who will receive services pursuant to section 280c(a) of this title and the average costs of providing such services to each such individual; and
a description of the manner in which such programs and activities will be coordinated with any similar programs and activities of public and private entities.

subsection.heading: Requirement of application
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary may not make a grant under section 280c(a) of this title to a State unless the State has submitted to the Secretary an application for the grant. The application shall—

paragraph.content: contain the description of intended expenditures required in subsection (b);
with respect to carrying out the purpose for which the grant is to be made, provide assurances of compliance satisfactory to the Secretary; and
otherwise be in such form, be made in such manner, and contain such information and agreements as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this subpart.
provide for an evaluation of each demonstration project for which a grant is made under section 280c(a) of this title; and
not later than 6 months after the completion of such evaluations, submit to the Congress a report describing the findings made as a result of the evaluations.

content.p: For the purpose of carrying out this subpart, there are authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 1988 through 1990, $7,500,000 for fiscal year 1991, and such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 1992 and 1993.

section.sourceCredit: (July 1, 1944, ch. 373, title III, § 397, as added Pub. L. 100–175, title VI, § 602, Nov. 29, 1987, 101 Stat. 980; amended Pub. L. 101–557, title I, § 101(d), Nov. 15, 1990, 104 Stat. 2766.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 397 of act July 1, 1944, ch. 373, title III, formerly § 399, as added Oct. 22, 1965, Pub. L. 89–291, § 2, 79 Stat. 1066; renumbered § 398 and amended Mar. 13, 1970, Pub. L. 91–212, §§ 2(g), 8, 10(c)(3), (d)(2)(A), 84 Stat. 63, 65, 67; June 18, 1973, Pub. L. 93–45, title I, § 107(f), 87 Stat. 92; renumbered § 397 and amended July 23, 1974, Pub. L. 93–353, title II, §§ 203(e), 204, 88 Stat. 372, 373, which related to grants to provide support for biomedical scientific publications, was classified to section 280b–9 of this title, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 99–158, § 3(b), Nov. 20, 1985, 99 Stat. 879.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1990—Subsec. (e). Pub. L. 101–557 substituted “there are” for “there is” and inserted before period at end “, $7,500,000 for fiscal year 1991, and such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 1992 and 1993”.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Oct. 1, 1987, see section 701(a) of Pub. L. 100–175, set out as an Effective Date of 1987 Amendment note under section 3001 of this title.
